background_template: {name} has been pulling shots in {location} since {founded}. Two grinders, a bench of regulars, and a queue that judges you for ordering slowly. {quirk}
neighbourhoods: a kiosk by the station underpass
the ground floor of the old print works
a corner unit opposite the park gates
a narrow shopfront on the high street
a converted garage down the mews
quirks: The espresso blend changes with the seasons and the regulars pretend not to notice.
There are exactly four tables and an unspoken rota for them.
The owner names each roast after a sea shanty and will not be talked out of it.
Oat milk quietly overtook dairy two winters ago and nobody mentions it.
The tip jar is a gravy boat. Nobody remembers why.
On Saturday mornings there is a dog in the doorway. It is not the owner's dog.
pains: people ring to ask if we have oat milk roughly nine times a day
the beans we sell by the bag change monthly and nobody knows what's in stock
we can't keep the menu board and our actual prices in step
people turn up at four expecting food we stop serving at two
half the internet still thinks we're closed on Mondays

products.name: Flat White
products.description: The one most people mean when they say coffee.

products.name: Espresso
products.description: Double, always. Singles are a myth.

products.name: Cortado
products.description: Half coffee, half milk, no debate.

products.name: Batch Filter
products.description: Brewed on the hour. Ask what's in the pot.

products.name: Single-Origin Pour-Over
products.description: Takes four minutes. Worth all four.

products.name: Mocha
products.description: Coffee for people who wanted hot chocolate.

products.name: Hot Chocolate
products.description: 54% dark, whipped cream optional but correct.

products.name: House Blend Beans, 250g
products.description: What we pull espresso with, in a bag.

products.name: Decaf Beans, 250g
products.description: Swiss water process. Tastes like coffee, honest.

products.name: Almond Croissant
products.description: From the bakery two doors down, in by eight.

awkward.name: Single-Origin Espresso — Finca El Mirador, Huila, Colombia (Washed, Seasonal Guest Lot)
awkward.description: The guest spot. Here for six weeks, then gone forever.

awkward.name: Cinnamon & Cardamom Bun
awkward.description: Gone by half ten most days.

features.name: menu listing
features.description: The whole menu — drinks, food, and the beans we sell by the bag, with prices. That's the point of the page.

features.name: contact form
features.description: Somewhere to ask about bulk bean orders or hiring the corner for a morning, without ringing during the rush.

features.name: opening hours
features.description: Hours, prominently. We are not closed on Mondays and haven't been for two years.

features.name: category filter
features.description: Let people jump to just the food, or just the beans, without scrolling the whole menu.

features.name: allergen information
features.description: What's in things — milk, nuts, gluten. We answer this forty times a day at the counter.

features.name: navigation
features.description: A way to move around the page. Nothing fancy.

constraints: It has to work on a phone. People check us from the bus stop over the road.
Our brand colour is #6F4E37. Please use it.
No stock photos of latte art. It's never our latte art.
Don't invent drinks. If it isn't on the board, it doesn't go on the page.
The prices on the page have to match the file we sent. To the penny.
Nothing that plays sound. It's a café, not an arcade.
